slides.title: An Introduction to Actor-Critic Methods
slides.subtitle: Understanding the Role of Actor-Critic in Reinforcement Learning

slides.title: What is Actor-Critic?
slides.content: Actor-Critic methods combine two types of reinforcement learning agents: the Actor, which decides which action to take, and the Critic, which evaluates the action based on a given policy. This setup helps in improving the stability and efficiency of learning.

slides.title: The Actor's Role
slides.content: The Actor is responsible for selecting actions based on the current policy. It updates the policy in the direction suggested by the Critic's feedback.

slides.title: The Critic's Role
slides.content: The Critic evaluates the actions taken by the Actor using a value function, which estimates the return or benefit of those actions.

slides.title: Advantages of Actor-Critic Methods
slides.content: Actor-Critic methods can converge faster than traditional methods. They leverage both the advantages of value-based and policy-based methods, providing a more stable learning process and better policy optimization.

slides.title: Visualizing Actor-Critic
slides.content: Let's look at a simple comparison of performance between different reinforcement learning methods including Actor-Critic.

slides.title: Conclusion
slides.content: Actor-Critic methods provide a robust framework in reinforcement learning by effectively utilizing both actors and critics. They are widely used in complex environments due to their balance of exploration and exploitation.
